name: 28 Days TWS
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a very stable 60fps and don't support HDR. While Pro renders at a slightly higher resolution, the difference in overall image quality is minimal. Shadows do however exhibit less flicker and certain assets (like workbenches) have more image stability on Pro.

name: ActionCam: SC
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a very stable 60fps. However, you can significantly increase the FOV, and it's here where Pro will have an easier time maintaining 60fps in more demanding scenes. Pro potentially has a slightly higher resolution than base PS5, but any difference is negligible - especially when the camera effects are toggled on.

name: Amer Fighting
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a locked 60fps. While both consoles also suffer from aliasing/shimmering alongside many assets with straight edges, these issues are worse on base PS5 (which also looks softer) due to its slightly lower internal resolution.

name: Amer: The Chase Hit & Run
pageTitle: Amer: The Chase Hit and Run
extraInfo: Any Pro enhancements are essentially imperceptible during general gameplay. For example, both base PS5 and Pro run at a locked 60fps, in SDR, with the same graphics presets for settings like LoDs, depth of field etc. Fortunately, both consoles also render a very 4K-like image (Pro could be slightly higher), with shimmering/aliasing only being particularly noticeable on traffic lights.

name: Highway Drifter: Hajwala
extraInfo: While Highway Drifter has the Pro Enhanced badge on the PSN Store, it's unclear what enhancements, if any, are actually present. Both base PS5 and Pro run at a locked 60fps. However, both consoles share image quality issues, including extreme pop-in, stair-stepped occlusion around the car on wet roads, and shimmering/instability on certain assets. Both also use SSR for reflections and seem to render at the same resolution, as they exhibit an identical number of stair-stepped edges in menus - something easy to verify due to no anti-aliasing. If there are Pro enhancements, they're minimal at best and base PS5 is easily capable of delivering the same experience.

name: Hole Digging Master
extraInfo: Both the Pro and base PS5 versions are the same for all intents and purposes. For example, the framerate is a locked 60fps on both. At best, Pro has a negligible increase in resolution that you'd find hard to notice even when comparing both versions side by side. Non-Pro enhanced games like Metaphor: ReFantazio showcase more significant improvements simply due to Pro's increased horsepower.

name: Lonely Hospital
extraInfo: The biggest difference between Pro and base PS5 is the framerate. While Pro runs at a basically locked 60fps, base PS5 hovers in the low 50s and regularly dips below the 48fps VRR window. Both consoles output a 4K-like image, however both exhibit significant noise/instability in reflections which suggests a low internal resolution. Pro's internal resolution is likely slightly higher as its noise is a bit more stable, but any resolution differences are minimal - the framerate stability is the true game changer.

name: Motel Business Simulator
extraInfo: Both the Pro and base PS5 version are the same for all intents and purposes. For example, the framerate is a locked 60fps, with no HDR support, and there's excessive shimmer on both consoles. At best, Pro has a negligible increase in resolution that you'd find hard to notice even when looking at both versions side by side. Non-Pro enhanced games like Metaphor: ReFantazio showcase more significant improvements simply due to Pro's increased horsepower.

name: OneTry
disclaimer: All tech information is based off Quisisana, which was Domynyo's previous game that had a similar genre/scope and therefore provides a good baseline of expectations until OneTry is tested:
extraInfo: 'Pro renders at 4K/60fps. However, there's (seemingly) no aliasing solution implemented, so edges of assets like doors/windows etc are still very jagged. Graphical settings such as lighting and some shadows are improved over Domynyo's previous game (Sanguis Luna). However, the differences in these graphical settings between the base PS5 and Pro version seem minimal.'

name: Only Up Rush
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a very stable 60fps. However, Pro has a slightly higher resolution, resulting in a cleaner/more 4K-like image, with less shimmer/instability in assets like railings/billboards/tarp.

name: Operation Candy Land
disclaimer: All info is taken from the demo and is subject to change for Operation Candy Land's launch.
extraInfo: Both base PS5 and Pro run at a locked 60fps. However, both render a soft 1080p-looking SDR image with noticeable shimmering/jaggies etc. If there are Pro enhancements, they're essentially imperceptible in general gameplay. Again, like the disclaimer states, a lack of meaningful enhancements could be down to it just being a demo of an unreleased game. However, considering the Pro enhancements (or lack thereof) in many other Mad Hook titles, anything meaningful is far from a guarantee.

name: Quisisana
extraInfo: Pro renders at 4K/60fps. However, there's (seemingly) no aliasing solution implemented, so edges of assets like doors/windows etc are still very jagged. Graphical settings such as lighting and some shadows are improved over Domynyo's previous game (Sanguis Luna). However, the differences in these graphical settings between the base PS5 and Pro version seem minimal.

name: Road Cafe Simulator
disclaimer: All tech information is based off Motel Business Simulator as there's no public information about Road Cafe Simulator's Pro enhancements. However, as both games are by Nostra, in the same genre, and have a similar aesthetic/scope, MBS provides a good baseline of what to expect from RCS:
extraInfo: 'Both the Pro and base PS5 version are the same for all intents and purposes. For example, the framerate is a locked 60fps, with no HDR support, and there's excessive shimmer on both consoles. At best, Pro has a negligible increase in resolution that you'd find hard to notice even when looking at both versions side by side. Non-Pro enhanced games like Metaphor: ReFantazio showcase more significant improvements simply due to Pro's increased horsepower.'

name: Sanguis Luna
extraInfo: Pro renders at 4K/60fps. However, there's (seemingly) no aliasing solution implemented, so edges of assets like doors/windows etc are still very jagged. Other graphical settings are also mediocre, such as missing shadows on basic assets like books. Therefore, the impact of any potential upgrades from base PS5 to Pro is minimal.

name: Weed & Greed
extraInfo: Both base PS5 and Pro run at a locked 60fps, and graphical settings like LODs are the same. However, Pro's internal resolution is slightly higher before outputting to 4K, leading to less shimmering on assets like railings and fewer sawtooth edges on shadows.
